title: Why we rewrote formsiiit in Go
author: anika.r
excerpt: Three years, two rewrites, and a lot of stack traces later — here's what we learned about choosing boring technology when half your contributors graduate every year.

title: Building cwdb without a spec
author: rohan.v
excerpt: We shipped a campus directory in six weeks. No spec, no designer, no manager. Here's how that went, and what we'd do differently.

title: The kernel reading group
author: priya.k
excerpt: We started reading the Linux kernel source together every Thursday. This is what we've learned so far, and why you should join us.

title: Open source in a closed campus
author: dev.m
excerpt: IIIT Hyderabad has a lot of great infrastructure. Most of it is closed source, undocumented, and breaks every semester. We're trying to fix that.

title: FOSS Meet 2026 recap
author: siddharth.a
excerpt: Four hundred people, twelve talks, and one very chaotic open-mic session. Here's what happened at this year's FOSS Meet.

title: Writing a static site generator in Rust
author: kartik.b
excerpt: monorail started as a weekend project and became something we actually use. Here's the journey from 'how hard can it be' to a tool with real users.

title: Good first issues that are actually good
author: anika.r
excerpt: We've been tagging issues for newcomers for two years. Here's what we learned about what makes an issue genuinely accessible, and what just looks like it is.

title: Grapevine and the problem of anonymous speech
author: rohan.v
excerpt: We built an anonymous message board. Then we had to decide what 'anonymous-but-accountable' actually means in practice. This is that story.
